name: PyBullet (Python Bindings for Bullet Physics Engine)
pricing_notes: Open source (zlib license); Erwin Coumans / Google

performance.notes: C++ Bullet backend; not GPU-accelerated for physics; OpenGL rendering

agent_readiness.notes: Python bindings for Bullet physics — popular for robotics RL. import pybullet as p. physicsClient = p.connect(p.DIRECT). p.setGravity(0, 0, -9.8). robotId = p.loadURDF('r2d2.urdf'). p.stepSimulation(). p.getBasePositionAndOrientation(robotId). Modes: GUI (OpenGL), DIRECT (headless). URDF/SDF/MJCF model loading. Gymnasium: pybullet_envs for environments. Contact detection. Constraints. Motor control. Soft body. Now largely superseded by MuJoCo for RL research.

gotchas: No type annotations — poor IDE support
API is functional (not OO) and can be confusing with multiple physics clients
Largely superseded by MuJoCo for RL research
Documentation is scattered — QuickStart PDF + examples + source
best_when: Legacy codebases using pybullet-based RL environments
When URDF model loading is needed with a free (zlib) license
Robotics simulation with soft body physics
Game physics where MuJoCo license restrictions matter
avoid_when: New RL robotics research (use MuJoCo 3 + Gymnasium)
When accuracy matters more than convenience
summary: PyBullet provides Python bindings for the Bullet physics engine. p.connect(p.DIRECT); p.loadURDF('robot.urdf'); p.stepSimulation(). URDF/SDF/MJCF models. GUI and headless modes. Contact detection, constraints, soft body. zlib license. No type annotations. Largely superseded by MuJoCo for RL research. Use for legacy pybullet environments or when URDF/soft body features are needed.
